incentive.statement: Preserve Andrews authority, typed source and target structure, hostile proof, single-runtime integrity, and browser verification while making every source-bearing Lesson 24-25 example executable through its actual grammar layer.

coordinator.responsibility: Coordinate stem formation, source voice, participant routing, later Canvas finalizers, presentation, and verification without turning Canvas targets into an expected-string authority table.

outcome: Every Lesson 24-25 source/result example is reproduced by a typed operation appropriate to its layer; formerly result-only records use explicit evidence-labeled typed source reconstructions and never target-string authority.

responsibilities.statement: Own productive and exact Lesson 24-25 causative formation, source analysis, participant transformations, ambiguity, and canonical validation.

actors.contribution: Close reusable rule gaps and add only bounded Andrews lexical overlays for unpredictable printed formations.

responsibilities.statement: Execute selected formation, source voice, object routing, and later finalization in canonical order.

actors.contribution: Preserve typed selection and distinguish source voice from later target voice.

responsibilities.statement: Expose genuine generated choices and layer ownership without constructing morphology.

actors.contribution: Render canonical options and precise blocked reasons for source-less evidence.

responsibilities.statement: Keep all capabilities in the canonical modern ESM graph.

actors.contribution: Preserve one runtime lane and installer-manifest parity.

responsibilities.statement: Maintain a section-indexed exhaustive coverage ledger and prove generated structure rather than expected-string lookup.

actors.contribution: Classify every Canvas example by layer, add hostile coverage, and report exact gaps.

responsibilities.statement: Keep target, board, shared action, evidence tags, alignment evidence, and closure state current.

actors.contribution: Prevent the earlier four-witness audit from serving as exhaustive completion proof.

retirements.removalEvidence: The exhaustive catalog proves 225/225 positive records exact and 2/2 negative constraints absent across all owning layers, with zero mismatches, missing records, or policy violations.

The audit first enumerates canonical signed possibilities into a generated-relation index. Printed targets only query that finished index; forged targets, source strings, and section labels cannot alter or authorize output.

evidence.result: All 227 catalog rows are classified: 225 positive rows reproduce their complete printed source and result exactly, and both negative rows independently prove their forbidden routes absent. Counts are 0 surface mismatches, 0 missing, and 0 policy violations.

Coverage is rebuilt by stem, perfective, valence, nonactive, VNC reading, VNC, ambiguity, mood, voice, stock, coalescence, synonym, and supplementation owners. The §25.13 quantity reading is a signed alternative-source projection and cannot mutate ordinary §25.12 generation.

The five source-unprinted records are explicitly Andrews-implied, Andrews-presupposed, or compositionally reconstructed. No result string supplies a source, option id, participant profile, or finalizer.

The exhaustive audit maps 227 evidence rows to 213 target-free generation keys, builds each canonical source/profile inventory into a shared generated-relation index, matches all Canvas rows only after generation, reports per-layer timing, and is reserved for lesson handoff. Ordinary tests keep representative and hostile checks.

A fresh-origin run of iuc-ci exposes Type 1 and Type 2, requires object type before relation, authorizes Tlaucxitia. and Quiucxitia. with exact formulas, resets relation when Change resets the object, preserves Linear/Diagram structure, and reports zero browser logs.
